---
title: Return Statistics
---

`q.stats` computes return-stream statistics with no Plotly dependency;
`q.plot` renders them. See [Indicators](../indicator/index.ipynb) for market
measurements and [Data](../data/index.qmd) for loading/saving OHLCV data.
Trade-log statistics
(`q.stats.trade_stats`, `q.stats.trades_to_returns`) are shown on the
[Trade logs](../plot/trades.ipynb) page.
Multiclass ROC and precision-recall curve tables are demonstrated alongside
their Plotly visualizations on the
[Classification curves](../plot/classification.ipynb) page.

Pages:

- [Risk estimators](risk.qmd) — historical, Gaussian, and entropic tail-loss
  measures with a consistent non-negative loss convention.
- [Netto Number](netto-number.qmd) — history, formula, risk-budget semantics,
  limitations, and adaptations for strategy research and reinforcement learning.
- [Performance & benchmark](performance.ipynb) — headline metrics
  (CAGR, Sharpe, Max Drawdown, ...) and benchmark-relative alpha/beta.
- [Rolling & calendar](rolling.ipynb) — rolling diagnostics and year-by-month
  return tables.
- [Factor analytics](factors.ipynb) — Fama-French-style multi-factor
  regression: full-period loadings, rolling betas, and return attribution.
- [Chained API](chained.ipynb) — `q.stats.returns(...)`, the bound
  stats-and-plots helper for notebook exploration.
